summary: Live hero (AmbientEditorialHero + AnatomyGlobeVisual) is architecturally sound but carries a single critical risk: the SVG viewBox (1600×1180) likely clips the pulse-line and inferior nodes (y≈952–1008) in standard viewport crops, sacrificing the most legible medical detail. The 851–900 logo batch is effectively a 2-glyph × 5-accent-color × 2-tagline matrix (~50 redundant variants); meaningful differentiation collapses to ~8 structurally distinct lockups. Frontpage 911–920 iterates three named medical concepts — Neural Veil (asymmetric blob, 911/915), Bronchial Sweep (bilateral lung ellipses, 913/917), Coronary Lattice (symmetric heart form with central axis, 920) — across minor weight/color variants. The hero object and BrandMark.tsx glyph use different visual vocabularies and share no explicit motif.
recommendations: {"id":"R1","priority":"ship-blocker","title":"Verify AnatomyGlobeVisual pulse-line is visible in deployed hero crop","detail":"The EKG pulse path (y≈952) and inferior nodes (y≈1008) sit in the bottom ~15% of the 1600×1180 viewBox. If hero-backdrop has overflow:hidden or a fixed height below 90vh on 1080p, these disappear. Open /app in browser, inspect hero-backdrop height, confirm the pulse path renders. If clipped, shift the pulse path up by ~120px or change the SVG preserveAspectRatio to xMidYMid meet instead of the likely xMidYMid slice.","file":"AnatomyGlobeVisual.tsx + globals.css .hero-backdrop"}

{"id":"R2","priority":"high","title":"Collapse 851–900 logo batch to 8 canonical lockups for outreach","detail":"The viable 8: (1) 851 — full neural-clinical glyph, complex, tagline \u0027NEURAL CLINICAL SYSTEM\u0027; (2) 855 — minimal glyph, teal #567C85, \u0027INSTRUMENT GRADE\u0027; (3) 860 — minimal glyph, gold #C49758, \u0027INSTRUMENT GRADE\u0027; (4) 865 — minimal glyph, sage #7F8F79, \u0027INSTRUMENT GRADE\u0027; (5) 870 — minimal glyph, terracotta #AD7E65, \u0027INSTRUMENT GRADE\u0027; (6) 876–878 range — check for any dark-field variant if exists; (7) 890 — terracotta, alternate weight; (8) 900 — gold, \u0027INSTRUMENT GRADE\u0027. Cut everything else. The teal (#567C85) minimal glyph variants (855/875) are the strongest match to the hero CTA and should be the outreach default. The BrandMark.tsx glyph (3-arch + axis + outer ring + apex node) is already cleaner than the 851–900 inner-ring variants — promote it as the live mark and archive the double-ring lockups.","file":"public/brand/options/ + BrandMark.tsx"}

{"id":"R3","priority":"high","title":"Top 3 frontpage concepts to advance: 920, 911, 913 — cut 914–919 duplicates","detail":"#1 — 920 (Coronary Lattice): strongest. Single dominant symmetric object with clear vertical axis, teal + gold dual-accent, \u0027Premium prep. Sharper bedside moves.\u0027 headline is the most conversion-direct of all 10. The central axis echoes BrandMark motif — best system coherence. #2 — 911 (Neural Veil, \u0027A calmer, more intelligent way to prepare.\u0027): the amorphous right-side blob is the closest to the live hero object; headline is empathetic and premium. #3 — 913 (Bronchial Sweep): bilateral lung ellipses are the most anatomically legible, but dual-object composition competes with text column — requires tighter left-margin discipline. Cut 912, 914–919 as duplicates. 915/917 are identical copy to 911/913 — they exist only as weight/color A/B variants; pick one per concept and delete the other.","file":"public/design-review/frontpage-911.svg, frontpage-913.svg, frontpage-920.svg"}

{"id":"R4","priority":"medium","title":"Bridge BrandMark.tsx glyph vocabulary to AnatomyGlobeVisual for system coherence","detail":"BrandMark uses a globe-with-arches motif (3 latitude arches, 1 vertical axis, 1 apex node). AnatomyGlobeVisual uses an asymmetric medical blob with meridians and accent branches. These share no explicit echo. Minimum viable fix: add a single `hero-medical-minor-line` arc in AnatomyGlobeVisual at the same curvature ratio as the BrandMark\u0027s top arch (currently d=\u0027M11.1 11.6 ... 20.9 11.6\u0027 maps to roughly a 155° arc at r≈6.9 in a 32px grid). This is a 1-path addition that ties the mark to the hero object without a full redesign.","file":"AnatomyGlobeVisual.tsx + BrandMark.tsx"}

skills: chapai-design-review: SVG hero objects use 1600-wide viewBox with right-anchored medical forms; always check bottom-15%-crop risk before shipping
chapai-logo-system: 851–900 batch is a color-variant matrix; canonical set is 8 lockups; live BrandMark.tsx is already the cleanest glyph
chapai-frontpage-concepts: three named directions — Neural Veil (blob), Bronchial Sweep (dual ellipse), Coronary Lattice (symmetric axis); Coronary Lattice is top-ranked for conversion clarity
durable_memory: Hero CTA color #567C85 (teal) is the primary accent; all logo shortlist picks should use this or gold #C49758 — no terracotta or sage for outreach-primary assets
BrandMark.tsx 3-arch globe glyph is live canonical mark; do not replace with double-ring variants from 851–900 batch
Frontpage copy anchor: 'Original questions, elite rationale, and a cleaner route into the right plan.' is locked across all 10 frontpage SVGs as body — do not iterate this line further
AmbientEditorialHero parallax: mouse lerp 0.12, scroll lerp 0.08, max scroll cap 720px — these values are already conservative and correct
active_context: Pre-outreach gate: hero crop validation (R1) must complete before any social share or screenshot asset is exported
Logo shortlist for outreach: 855 (teal) as primary, 860 (gold) as secondary, 851 (complex) as brand depth reference
Frontpage direction for next dev sprint: Coronary Lattice (920) → implement as live page variant or A/B test against current Neural Veil hero

current_blocker: Cannot confirm whether the AnatomyGlobeVisual pulse-line and inferior nodes render in the deployed hero without inspecting live CSS — globals.css was unreadable at 72k tokens. The hero may be shipping with its most legible medical detail clipped.
suggested_human_fix: Open the live site (or localhost) in Chrome, inspect .hero-backdrop, confirm rendered height ≥ 900px at 1080p, and visually verify the EKG pulse path at the bottom of the SVG is visible. If clipped, grep globals.css for 'hero-backdrop' and share the height/overflow values — the fix is a 2-line CSS change.
next_task: Implement Coronary Lattice (frontpage-920) as a second hero variant in AmbientEditorialHero via a new `variant` prop ('neural-veil' | 'coronary-lattice'), swap the AnatomyGlobeVisual for a CoronaryLatticeVisual component mirroring the 920 SVG paths, and wire a URL param (?v=cl) for side-by-side review before outreach begins.
